title: The One Where It All Began

content.problemStatement:
In a New York City coffee house called Central Perk, we're introduced to six friends: chef Monica Geller, data controller Chandler Bing who lives across the hall from Monica, Chandler's roommate/actor Joey Tribbiani, Monica's brother Ross Geller, new ageist Phoebe Buffay, and "born to be a wife" Rachel Green.

Chef wants to divide NNN cookies between the **six** friends such that:


Each friend gets at least one cookie.
No two friends get the same number of cookies.
Find if it is possible for Chef to do so.


Input Format

First line will contain TTT, number of testcases. Then the testcases follow.
Each testcase contains of a single line of input, one integers NNN.


Output Format
For each test case print "YES" if Chef can divide the cookies according to the given rules, else print "NO".


You may print each character of each string in uppercase or lowercase (for example, the strings "yEs", "yes", "Yes" and "YES" will all be treated as identical).
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer N, the number of cookies.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing "YES" if it is possible to distribute the N cookies among the six friends according to the rules, otherwise output "NO".

sampleTestCases.explanation: 42 >= 21 so YES. 5 < 21 so NO. 909 >= 21 so YES.

solution.approach: The smallest possible sum of six distinct positive integers is 1+2+3+4+5+6 = 21. If N is at least 21 we can always construct a valid distribution: start with the set {1,2,3,4,5,6} (sum 21) and add the remaining N-21 cookies to the largest element (6). The new largest element becomes 6+(N-21) which is still larger than the other five numbers, so all six numbers remain distinct and positive. Therefore the answer is YES iff N >= 21, otherwise NO.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – each test case is processed in constant time.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used.
